questions.q: What kind of object is the Chudamani?
questions.explanation: The Chudamani is a hair ornament, a crest-jewel that Sita wore on her head.

questions.q: Who gave the Chudamani to be carried back to Rama?
questions.explanation: Sita entrusted her crest-jewel to be taken to Rama as proof she still lived.

questions.q: To whom did Sita give the Chudamani to carry away?
questions.explanation: Sita handed the jewel to Hanuman when he met her in the Ashoka grove.

questions.q: Where was Sita held when she gave the Chudamani to Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Sita was kept captive in the Ashoka grove (Ashoka Vatika) in Lanka.

questions.q: For whom was the Chudamani ultimately intended?
questions.explanation: The jewel was a token meant to reach Rama, Sita's husband.

questions.q: What did the Chudamani serve as proof of?
questions.explanation: The jewel was a sign to Rama that Sita still lived and endured her captivity.

questions.q: Who had abducted Sita and held her in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka, abducted Sita and held her captive.

questions.q: What token had Hanuman first shown Sita to prove he came from Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman showed Sita Rama's signet ring, after which she gave him her crest-jewel in return.

questions.q: On what part of the body is a chudamani worn?
questions.explanation: A chudamani is a jewel worn in the hair or on the crown of the head.

questions.q: Hanuman is the son of which deity?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the son of Vayu, the wind god, which gave him his great power of flight.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Valmiki Ramayana does Sita give Hanuman the Chudamani?
questions.explanation: The episode occurs in the Sundara Kanda, which centers on Hanuman's journey to Lanka.

questions.q: Why did Sita send a personal jewel rather than just a verbal message?
options: To pay Hanuman for his help
As tangible proof that the messenger truly reached her
Because she had no words to send
questions.explanation: A physical, recognizable jewel served as undeniable proof to Rama that Hanuman had genuinely found Sita.

questions.q: Along with the jewel, Sita recounted a private incident to Rama. Which event was it?
options: The crow (Jayanta) that wounded her, struck by Rama's blade of grass
The building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Sita recalled the Kakasura (crow) episode, a private memory only she and Rama shared, to confirm the message's authenticity.

questions.q: From where on her person did Sita reportedly take the Chudamani to give to Hanuman?
options: From a hidden pouch in her garment
From her hair, where she had kept it concealed
From a box buried in the grove
From a guard who returned it
questions.explanation: Sita had kept the crest-jewel tucked in her hair (or garment) throughout her captivity and drew it out for Hanuman.

questions.q: How did Rama react when Hanuman presented the Chudamani to him?
options: He was overcome with emotion and tears, recognizing it
He refused to believe it was hers
questions.explanation: Rama was deeply moved to tears on seeing and recognizing Sita's familiar jewel.

questions.q: According to tradition, the Chudamani had originally been given to Sita on which occasion?
options: At her wedding / by her father Janaka's family
questions.explanation: The crest-jewel was a treasured ornament Sita had since her marriage, associated with her father Janaka's household.

questions.q: What was Hanuman's chief purpose in journeying to Lanka before receiving the jewel?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's mission was to search for and find Sita, confirming her whereabouts for Rama and the army.

questions.q: Which monkey king's forces had organized the search that sent Hanuman to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Sugriva, king of the vanaras after Vali, dispatched the search parties to find Sita.

questions.q: Where exactly within the Ashoka grove did Hanuman observe Sita before approaching her?
options: Beneath a Shimshapa (Ashoka) tree, guarded by rakshasis
questions.explanation: Hanuman watched Sita seated beneath a tree in the grove, surrounded by guarding rakshasi women.

questions.q: What did Hanuman offer to do for Sita, which she declined, choosing to send the jewel instead?
options: To carry her back to Rama on his shoulders at once
To free all the other captives
To bring Rama secretly into Lanka
questions.explanation: Hanuman offered to carry Sita to Rama immediately, but she declined, preferring Rama win her honorably; she sent the jewel as her token.

questions.q: The Chudamani primarily symbolizes which quality of Sita in the story?
options: Her desire to stay in Lanka
questions.explanation: The jewel stands as a token of Sita's endurance and unwavering devotion during her captivity.

questions.q: In the card's collection, the Chudamani is classified as which type of entry?
questions.explanation: The Chudamani card is categorized as an artifact, an object of significance in the Ramayana.

questions.q: After receiving the jewel and message, what did Hanuman do before leaving Lanka?
options: He let himself be captured and burned the city, then departed
He negotiated Sita's release with Ravana
He waited for Rama's army to arrive
questions.explanation: Hanuman went on to confront the rakshasas, was captured, and set Lanka ablaze before returning to Rama with the jewel and news.

questions.q: The word 'chudamani' literally combines which meanings in Sanskrit?
questions.explanation: 'Chuda' refers to the crest or topknot of the head and 'mani' means jewel, so chudamani means crest-jewel.

questions.q: Which of these is the strongest reason the Chudamani convinced Rama, beyond mere recognition of an ornament?
options: It was magically inscribed with Rama's name
Sita paired it with a private memory only she and Rama knew
Ravana had signed a note with it
questions.explanation: The jewel together with the secret Kakasura memory removed any doubt, since only the true Sita could relate that private event.

questions.q: In the sequence of mutual tokens, which exchange order is correct?
options: Sita gives jewel first, then Hanuman shows the ring
Hanuman shows Rama's ring first, then Sita gives her jewel
Rama gives the jewel, Sita keeps the ring
questions.explanation: Hanuman first presented Rama's signet ring to win Sita's trust, and only then did Sita give him her crest-jewel to carry back.

questions.q: Whom did Hanuman address and reassure with Rama's message right before Sita gave the jewel?
questions.explanation: Hanuman consoled the grieving Sita with Rama's words and assurance of rescue, after which she entrusted him with the jewel.

questions.q: What did Sita ask Hanuman to convey to Rama regarding her remaining time?
options: That she could endure only a limited time before despair
That she wished to remain in Lanka
That she no longer loved Rama
questions.explanation: Sita pleaded that Rama come swiftly, saying she could endure her captivity only a short while longer.

questions.q: The Chudamani episode is pivotal because it transforms Rama's quest from uncertainty into what?
options: A confirmed rescue mission with a known location
questions.explanation: Hanuman's return with the jewel and Sita's exact location turned the search into a definite campaign to rescue her in Lanka.

questions.q: Which statement best distinguishes the Chudamani from Rama's signet ring in the narrative?
options: The ring went from Rama to Sita; the jewel went from Sita to Rama
Both were sent by Rama to Sita
Both were sent by Sita to Rama
The jewel was sent first, the ring never delivered
questions.explanation: Rama's ring traveled outward (Rama to Sita via Hanuman) while the Chudamani traveled back (Sita to Rama via Hanuman).
